Off Calendar Jun-26-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT WALTER DAWYDIAK Motion for Order Vacating and Setting Aside Judgment. Appearances: Philip Horne, Esq. (Attorney at Law), (415) 504-4573, Plaintiff and co-counsel for Plaintiff appearing and opposing party; Walter Dawydiak, Defendant in Pro Per, and moving party, appearing only for himself, not the corporation. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. Insufficient showing of clerical error by the court. (CCP section 473(d).) Defendant did not bring this motion within a reasonable time after judgment was entered. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Second Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel The Taking Of The Deposition Of Defendant Walter Dawydiak And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-25-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Joyce Jordan for plaintiff and moving party Phil Horne
Brooke P. Halsey Jr. for defendant and opposing party Cars Dawydiak Inc.

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Phil Horne's motion to compel the deposition of Walter Dawydiak. The motion is moot as defendant has appeared for his deposition. However the Court finds that defendant failed to make a reasonable and good faith effort to informally resolve the matter in dispute thereby requiring the filing of the instant motion. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1615 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Second Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendant Cars Dawydiak, Inc.'S Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-25-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Joyce Jordan for plaintiff and moving party Phil Horne
Brooke P. Halsey Jr. for defendant and opposing party Cars Dawydiak Inc.

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Phil Horne's motion to compel further production of documents is Granted.
Defendant is ordered to produce all documents responsive to requests #3, 4, and 14 within 7 days of notice of this order. If no further documents exist than what was previously produced during deposition, defendant shall verify that representation in a declaration that complies with CCP Section 2031.230. The request for monetary sanctions is denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
